lede: A look at October 13 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Caleb McLaughlin and Ian Thorpe, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Brigid Kosgei
events.desc: Kenyan Brigid Kosgei sets a new world record for a woman runner with a time of 2:14:04 at the 2019 Chicago Marathon.

events.title: Michel Aoun
events.desc: Syrian forces attack free areas of Lebanon, removing General Michel Aoun from the presidential palace.

events.title: Uruguayan Air Force Flight 571
events.desc: Uruguayan Air Force Flight 571 crashes in the Andes mountains. Twenty-eight survive the crash. All but 16 succumb before rescue on December 23.

events.title: Aeroflot Flight 217
events.desc: Aeroflot Flight 217 crashes outside Moscow, killing 174.

events.title: Ankara
events.desc: Ankara becomes the capital of Turkey.

events.title: Miracle of the Sun
events.desc: The "Miracle of the Sun" is witnessed by an estimated 70,000 people in the Cova da Iria in Portugal.

events.title: Prince Arthur, Duke of Connaught and Strathearn
events.desc: Prince Arthur, Duke of Connaught and Strathearn, becomes the first Governor General of Canada of royal descent.

events.title: 206P/Barnard–Boattini
events.desc: Edward Emerson Barnard is first to discover a comet by photographic means.

events.title: First Battle of Wissembourg
events.desc: French Revolutionary Wars: Austro-Prussian victory over Republican France at the First Battle of Wissembourg.

events.title: Henry IV of England
events.desc: Coronation of Henry IV of England at Westminster Abbey.

births.name: Lacy J. Dalton
births.line: American country music singer-songwriter and guitarist

deaths.text: Mayra Gómez Kemp, Cuban-Spanish television host and actress
Takashi Yanase, Japanese poet and illustrator, created Anpanman
Louis Rwagasore, Burundi politician, Prime Minister of Burundi

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Alexandrina of Balasar
Christian feast day: Daniel and companions, of Ceuta
